reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome end-to-end run (WebGPU AMD RDNA-2 sandbox) + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Driven headless run (fresh profile, served copy): loader showed a single 'Download model (~265 MB)' button with no silent transfer, clicked Download → model loaded on WebGPU → clicked Convert on the bundled report sample → real DocTags stream '<doctag><section_header_level_1><loc_44><loc_33>…Northwind Trading -Q2 Report</section_header_level_1><text>…Prepared by the Finance Office · 30 June 2026</text><text>…The company recorded strong performance in the second quarter…</text>…' parsed into 8 structured elements; page split into 17 tiles. Console + network clean (0 errors). Source loads AutoProcessor + AutoModelForVision2Seq in a Web Worker and streams the model's own DocTags — no canned result.
rubric.notes: Verified runnable BEFORE building: docling-project/SmolDocling-256M-preview loads ONLY under Transformers.js v4 (in 3.7.5 the ONNX session build aborts with a bare numeric error — A/B tested vs SmolVLM v1 which DOES load on 3.7.5), so the worker pins v4.2.0 locally like SAM2. dtype q4 was chosen by measurement: q4f16 / fp16 / int8 all emit repeated-token garbage on this model; q4 produced coherent DocTags on all three samples.

rubric.evidence: Output is DocTags — the expected structured markup — with per-element bounding boxes, verified on three samples at the model level: the report → section_header + text elements with <loc_> boxes; the table → <otsl> with <ched> column headers (Region/Units/Revenue/Growth) and <fcel> cells (North 1,240 312,000 … West 760 190,300) + <nl> row breaks; the formula page → <formula> as LaTeX 'f ( x ) = ( 1 / ( \sigma \sqrt ( 2 \pi ) ) ) \cdot e ^ { - ( x - \mu ) ^ { 2 } / ( 2 \sigma ^ { 2 } )'. The page parses DocTags into an element list (type + box + text/table rows).
rubric.notes: Expected shape (document image → DocTags: layout + text + OTSL tables + LaTeX formulas with boxes) is correct and legibly surfaced. Semantic sanity confirmed against the known sample content.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (docling-project/SmolDocling-256M-preview linked to card), declares-task (image-text-to-text), declares-backend (WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the idefics3 architecture, the DocTags output vocab, dtype q4, AND explicitly discloses the two non-standard facts: the v4.2.0 local version-pin (3.x can't load its ONNX) and that q4f16/fp16/int8 are degenerate so q4 is the honest export.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype/version match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu, dtype q4, Transformers.js 4.2.0 pinned locally). Honest, complete disclosure incl. the CDLA-Permissive-2.0 license.

rubric.evidence: First visit: loader in 'download-required' with a single 'Download model (~265 MB)' button — no silent transfer (verified coldButtons == ['Download model (~265 MB)']). Warm reload in the same profile: loader auto-inits to 'ready' with the ONLY action button being 'Clear cached model' (verified warmButtons == ['Clear cached model'], no Download). Uses the shared createModelLoader; lib/model-cache is version-agnostic so the v4-pinned worker still auto-inits from cache.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; cached-current → auto-init, no button; per-model clear-cache present.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Convert shows 'Converting…' then 'Done.'; on a WebGPU-absent device the loader's requiresWebGPU gate + the page's onError handler render an explicit needs-WebGPU panel (webgpuFallbackHTML) with enable steps — never fake output.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. This is a WebGPU-only model: on a no-GPU device it shows the honest needs-WebGPU state (blocked, not a pass). Offline-cached reuse + forced partial/evicted cache states not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: 3 sample thumbnails (keyboard-activatable role=button), drop/upload zone, prompt input, quick-prompt chips, Convert, max-tokens. Basics: sample picker + Convert + structure list. Practical: Convert + Copy Markdown + readonly Markdown textarea. Wild: table/formula sample picker + Extract (renders OTSL tables + LaTeX). Multi-model: two loaders + Read & summarise. Buttons have accessible names; thumbnails carry alt text + keydown handlers.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the structure/raw panels show only instructional placeholders; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The visible DocTags + parsed structure were produced by the on-device WebGPU run (8 elements, 17 tiles observed). On a no-GPU device the page shows the labelled needs-WebGPU state, not a canned document.
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the raw DocTags stream is shown verbatim (including minor model quirks like a spurious leading superscript on the table sample) rather than being cleaned up to look perfect.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (preview + sample thumbnails carry alt), buttons-have-names. Status + structure + raw stream use aria-live; sample thumbnails are role=button + tabindex=0 with Enter/Space handlers and focus-visible outlines; the dropzone is a labelled role=button.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming DocTags and the parsed structure list would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on the main page: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280×800 (driven end-to-end) AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch, light+dark screenshots read — no overflow, legible). doc-grid uses flex-wrap+gap with min-inline-size:0; preview/textarea/struct-table set max-inline-size:100%; the raw DocTags + Markdown panels scroll inside their own containers; struct-table wraps. Sub-pages (basics/practical/wild) driven cold: no overflow, zero console errors.
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). All five pages share the same doc-grid + DOCLING_CSS patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (the VLM forward pass + autoregressive DocTags generation) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the 256M-param model never blocks the main thread; tokens stream to the UI incrementally. The multi-model page runs SmolDocling and the summariser LLM in separate workers. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture + streaming).
rubric.notes: A ~45 s full-page conversion on the no-GPU-optimised sandbox is expected; on a real GPU it is much faster. An explicit INP measurement during streaming would confirm; off-thread + incremental streaming are the key mitigations.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow, wide code blocks and tables scroll internally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap+gap and intrinsic sizing; set min-inline-size:0 / max-inline-size:100% on flex items and media so long content can't force horizontal overflow; keep wide content (raw DocTags, tables, code) scrolling inside its own container.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — doc-grid uses flex-wrap+gap with min-inline-size:0 on both columns; preview/textarea set max-inline-size:100%; the .doctags-raw stream and the Markdown textarea scroll inside their own overflow:auto boxes; struct-table is width:100% with wrapping cells; the API <pre> scrolls. Verified no horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflowDesktop true; mobile light+dark screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live for streaming output
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep image thumbnails keyboard-operable with alt text and visible focus.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; the structure + raw DocTags panels use aria-live; sample thumbnails are role=button tabindex=0 with Enter/Space handlers + focus-visible; the dropzone is a labelled role=button.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass; DOM inspection

guidanceConsulted.query: stream tokens/generation without INP regressions long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP; stream results incrementally.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the VLM forward pass + DocTags generation run in models/smoldocling-document/worker.js (v4-pinned); tokens stream to the UI via postMessage; the multi-model summariser runs in its own worker. The main thread only renders incoming tags.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: detect WebGPU capability and degrade gracefully
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Feature-detect the required capability and show an explicit, actionable unsupported state rather than a broken or fake UI.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — createModelLoader gates on a real WebGPU adapter probe (requiresWebGPU:true); the worker also has an independent probeGPU; on failure the page renders webgpuFallbackHTML with the real reason + chrome://gpu / flags enable steps — never a fake document.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker probeGPU + loader requiresWebGPU gate + gpuFallback panel; honest needs-WebGPU state

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the sample thumbnails + copy-Markdown button operate correctly?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run).

Multi-model chain verified in parts (SmolDocling → Markdown driven end-to-end producing '## Northwind Trading -Q2 Report…'; the Qwen2.5-0.5B summariser verified in isolation on that Markdown → an accurate multi-sentence brief). The summariser uses dtype q4 + sampling: the q4f16 export and greedy decoding both degenerate on this WebGPU backend, so the worker pins q4 + the qwen-tiny-llm sampling config. Remaining: a single driven pass with both models resident simultaneously + combined-latency measurement.
Measure real INP during DocTags streaming to confirm the off-thread architecture keeps interactions snappy.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a DocTags → HTML / JSON export alongside the Markdown export (practical), as a NEW control — DocTags carry enough structure for both.
Overlay the <loc_> bounding boxes back onto the source page image so users can see exactly where each extracted element came from.
Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for smoldocling-document across all 5 pages.

summary: smoldocling-document is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: SmolDocling-256M converts a document IMAGE into DocTags — structured markup with per-element bounding boxes, OTSL tables and LaTeX formulas — materially different from the built Nougat (Markdown), Donut (doc-VQA) and TrOCR (line OCR) demos. Verified runnable BEFORE building: the idefics3 ONNX loads ONLY under Transformers.js v4 (A/B tested — 3.7.5 aborts), so the worker pins v4.2.0 locally like the SAM2 precedent; dtype q4 was chosen by measurement (q4f16/fp16/int8 are all degenerate for this model). Driven end-to-end in headless Chrome on a real WebGPU adapter: fresh profile shows a labelled ~265 MB Download with no silent transfer, a real DocTags stream parsed into 8 structured elements over a 17-tile page, warm reload auto-inits to ready with only a Clear-cache button, clean console, no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile. Table + formula extraction verified at the model level (OTSL cells; LaTeX). Five pages: basics (page → structure), practical (DocTags → copyable Markdown), wild (table + formula extraction), multi-model (SmolDocling → Qwen2.5-0.5B brief — summariser pinned to q4 + sampling after the q4f16/greedy path was measured degenerate; verified in parts). WebGPU-only: degrades to an honest needs-WebGPU state, never a fake. Remaining work is a keyboard/SR traversal, explicit cache-state drives, and a bounding-box overlay. Revision 1.
